name: Gustavo Petro
role: President of Colombia (2022–present); former M-19 member; first leftist president in Colombian history
quote: La paz total no es una utopía, es una necesidad histórica de Colombia. No más guerra, no más violencia.

name: Álvaro Uribe Vélez
role: President of Colombia (2002–2010); architect of 'Democratic Security' policy; founder of Centro Democrático party; most influential figure in post-2000 Colombian politics
quote: Con la seguridad democrática derrotamos al terrorismo sin sacrificar las libertades democráticas.

name: Juan Manuel Santos
role: President of Colombia (2010–2018); architect of 2016 FARC peace accord; Nobel Peace Prize laureate 2016
quote: La paz es el reto más difícil que ha tenido Colombia, pero también es la tarea más urgente de mi generación.

name: Simón Bolívar
role: Liberator of South America; President of Gran Colombia (1819–1830); won decisive Battle of Boyacá (1819); died in Santa Marta, December 1830
quote: Un pueblo ignorante es un instrumento ciego de su propia destrucción.

name: Pablo Escobar
role: Leader of Medellín Cartel (1976–1993); controlled ~80% of global cocaine trade; killed by Colombian security forces, 2 December 1993
quote: Prefiero una tumba en Colombia que una cárcel en Estados Unidos.

name: Rodrigo Londoño ('Timochenko')
role: Last FARC-EP commander-in-chief; signed 2016 peace accord; political party leader of Comunes; underwent health treatment after signing
quote: Pedimos perdón a todas las víctimas del conflicto. Este es el comienzo de una nueva Colombia.

name: Manuel Marulanda Vélez ('Tirofijo')
role: FARC-EP founder and commander for over 40 years; died of natural causes March 2008; led FARC from Marquetalia (1964) to mass organization
quote: Las armas son la única respuesta a un Estado que solo responde al pueblo con la violencia.

name: Iván Márquez
role: Former FARC peace negotiator; chief negotiator in Havana (2012–2016); declared return to arms August 2019; founded Segunda Marquetalia
quote: El Estado incumplió el acuerdo. Volvemos a la montaña porque nos han dejado sin opciones.

name: Jorge Eliécer Gaitán
role: Liberal populist leader; mayor of Bogotá; justice minister; frontrunner for 1950 presidency; assassinated 9 April 1948, triggering the Bogotazo
quote: Yo no soy un hombre, soy un pueblo. El pueblo es superior a sus dirigentes.

name: Carlos Castaño Gil
role: Co-founder and leader of AUC (United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia); responsible for numerous massacres; disappeared/killed 2004, allegedly by own organization
quote: Somos la respuesta armada del Estado que no puede con la guerrilla.

name: Francia Márquez
role: Vice President of Colombia (2022–present); Afro-Colombian environmental and human rights activist; Goldman Environmental Prize laureate 2018; first Afro-Colombian VP
quote: Estamos luchando para que el Buen Vivir sea posible para todas y todos, para que no haya más muertos.

name: Gonzalo Jiménez de Quesada
role: Spanish conquistador; led expedition from Santa Marta up Magdalena River (1536–1538); conquered Muisca confederation; founded Bogotá (1538)
quote: En nombre de Su Majestad el Rey de España, tomo posesión de estas tierras y sus gentes.

name: Rodrigo Lara Bonilla
role: Colombian Justice Minister (1983–1984); aggressively pursued Medellín Cartel; exposed Escobar's narco-money in Congress; assassinated by cartel, 30 April 1984
quote: El narcotráfico no tiene amigos en este gobierno. Vamos a combatirlo sin tregua ni descanso.

name: Gustavo Rojas Pinilla
role: Colombian military dictator (1953–1957); ousted Laureano Gómez; briefly reduced partisan violence; forced out by civic revolt; ran for president as ANAPO candidate (disputed loss, 1970)
quote: Paz, justicia y libertad: esas son las tres bases de mi gobierno.

name: Iván Cepeda Castro
role: Colombian Senator (Polo Democrático / Pacto Histórico); human rights lawyer; led investigations into false positives and paramilitarism; son of journalist Manuel Cepeda murdered by paramilitaries in 1994
quote: La impunidad es el mayor obstáculo para la paz en Colombia. Sin verdad no puede haber reconciliación.

name: Andrés Pastrana Arango
role: President of Colombia (1998–2002); established 42,000 km² Caguán demilitarized zone for FARC peace talks that collapsed February 2002; launched Plan Colombia with US support; son of former president Misael Pastrana
quote: Las FARC utilizaron la zona de distensión como cuartel militar. Se me acabó la paciencia y le puse fin al proceso.

name: Ernesto Samper Pizano
role: President of Colombia (1994–1998); center of 'Proceso 8000' scandal — Cali Cartel admitted to funding his campaign with ~$6M; US revoked his visa and decertified Colombia; survived impeachment in Congress (111-43 acquittal)
quote: Los dineros del narcotráfico entraron a mi campaña sin mi conocimiento ni consentimiento.

name: Antonio Nariño
role: Colombian independence precursor (1765–1823); translated and secretly printed the French Declaration of the Rights of Man (1794); arrested and exiled by Spanish crown; fought alongside Bolívar; called the 'Precursor of Colombian Independence'
quote: Traduje los Derechos del Hombre para que el pueblo colombiano supiera que tenía derechos que nadie le podía arrebatar.

name: Piedad Córdoba
role: Colombian senator (Liberal/Polo Democrático); Afro-Colombian human rights advocate; brokered high-profile FARC hostage releases (2007–2009) including Clara Rojas; expelled from Senate 2010 by Procurador General for alleged FARC collaboration; maintained pro-Venezuela/Cuba positions
quote: No hay paz sin justicia, y no hay justicia sin la verdad completa sobre lo que ha pasado en Colombia.

name: Iván Duque Márquez
role: President of Colombia (2018–2022); Centro Democrático (Uribe's chosen successor); attempted to modify JEP provisions; faced massive 2021 Paro Nacional protests over tax reform and police violence; extradited Otoniel to US May 2022
quote: Mi gobierno se sustenta en tres pilares: legalidad, emprendimiento y equidad. Sin legalidad no hay prosperidad posible.

name: María de los Ángeles Cano Márquez
role: Colombia's first major female political leader (1887–1967); 'La Flor del Trabajo' — organized workers and peasants across Colombia 1925–1930; co-founded the Revolutionary Socialist Party (PSR); imprisoned and politically silenced by Conservative-Liberal repression after 1930
quote: La mujer colombiana ya despertó. No pedimos permiso para luchar por nuestros derechos: los tomamos.

name: Laureano Gómez Castro
role: President of Colombia (1950–1953); Conservative ideologue and authoritarian; his extreme rule intensified La Violencia against Liberals; sympathetic to Franco's Spain; overthrown by Rojas Pinilla; co-architect of the National Front power-sharing pact (1958) from exile in Spain
quote: El liberalismo es una herejía política. Colombia necesita un Estado ordenado bajo los principios de la civilización cristiana.

name: Dairo Antonio Úsuga ('Otoniel')
role: Former supreme leader of Clan del Golfo (AGC), Colombia's largest criminal organization (~8,000 armed members); ex-FARC/EPL guerrilla turned narco-paramilitary; captured 23 October 2021 in Urabá by 500 special forces troops; extradited to US May 2022 on drug trafficking charges
quote: Me entrego para contribuir a la paz de Colombia. Hay muchas verdades que el país necesita conocer.
